Title: Business sentiment slides as geopolitical risks bite: RAM survey
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian business sentiment has weakened further in the second quarter of 2026 as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East raised costs and disrupted supply chains, according to RAM Ratings.
Its Business Confidence Index fell to 33.4 in the second quarter from 42.2 in the first quarter, marking the second straight quarter of pessimistic business sentiment.
The decline was broad-based, with weaker expectations for sales, profitability and capital investment driving the fall.
The survey, conducted between June 10 and June 25, found that cost pressures had become the top concern for businesses, with 68 per cent of respondents identifying them as a key challenge, up from 57 per cent in the first quarter.
Concerns over weak economic conditions remained high, although they eased slightly to 66 per cent from 71 per cent previously.
Meanwhile, worries over supply chain disruptions more than doubled, with 18 per cent of firms citing the issue compared with 8 per cent in the previous quarter.
This reflects uncertainty over the availability and cost of production inputs.
A special survey on the impact of the Middle East conflict found that 82 per cent of businesses experienced higher operating costs, with nearly half reporting cost increases of 10 per cent or more.
Logistics and shipping were the hardest-hit cost components, cited by 69 per cent of respondents, followed by raw materials (64 per cent) and fuel and energy costs (50 per cent).
Businesses were almost evenly split on how they responded to the higher costs.
About 51 per cent passed on part of the increase to customers through higher prices, while 49 per cent chose to absorb the additional costs to remain competitive.
Besides price adjustments, 45 per cent of respondents implemented operational changes, such as switching suppliers, streamlining inventories or modifying business processes, while around 25 per cent delayed workforce expansion.
Among firms that had not raised prices, one-third said they were concerned about losing customers, underscoring weak demand and intense market competition.
RAM Holdings Bhd group chief executive officer and executive director Chris W.K. Lee said the survey showed that the conflict in the Middle East had pushed up business costs through supply chain, logistics and energy channels.
"The continued disruption at the Strait of Hormuz may continue to pressure costs going forward. Any alleviation in business pressures in the short term is unlikely to be immediate and complete.
"Continued assistance will still be needed to help firms adapt and weather the lingering turbulence," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Capital A's ADE gains recognition at MAIA's President Award
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asia Digital Engineering (ADE), the maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) arm of Capital A, has won two awards at the MAIA President's Award 2026.
ADE received the Leadership Competencies and Development Award under the corporate category, in recognition of its efforts in leadership development, talent cultivation and workforce empowerment.
Meanwhile, ADE chief executive officer Mahesh Kumar was presented with the Aerospace Premiership Award, which honours individuals who have demonstrated leadership, innovation and contributions to the aerospace sector.
The awards, introduced by the Malaysia Aerospace Industry Association (MAIA), recognise aerospace companies and individuals that have showed excellence in management practices, product and service quality and stakeholder value creation.
The awards were presented by Investment, Trade and Industry Deputy Minister Sim Tze Tzin and MAIA president Naguib Mohd Nor at a ceremony in Kuala Lumpur.
Mahesh said the recognition reflects ADE's commitment to building a culture of excellence, innovation and continuous learning while developing world-class aerospace talent.
"Developing world-class aerospace talent is an investment not only in our company, but also in Malaysia's future as a leading aerospace and MRO hub.
"As we grow, we remain committed to strengthening the nation's MRO capabilities, creating high-value opportunities for local talent, and positioning Malaysia among the world's leading aerospace maintenance destinations," he said.
Naguib said ADE stood out among this year's nominees for its leadership, innovation and talent development initiatives, which have helped transform the company into a regional MRO player.
He said the company's achievements highlight the growing strength of Malaysia's aerospace industry and its potential to compete on the global stage.
The MAIA President's Award recognises organisations and individuals that demonstrate excellence, innovation and leadership across Malaysia's aerospace sector, with winners selected through an evaluation process conducted by an independent panel of industry experts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police bust syndicate using Malaysia as processing hub for MDMA vape cartridges
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have busted a foreigner-run drug syndicate allegedly using Malaysia as a processing and packaging hub to smuggle Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA)-laced vape cartridges to Indonesia via maritime routes.
Federal police Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the operation, codenamed Op Pijat, led to the arrest of eight foreigners — six men and two women aged between 21 and 40 — in raids in Meru and Kajang, Selangor.
"We also seized 500 plastic packets containing vape cartridges suspected to contain MDMA, weighing nearly 5kg, as well as eight small plastic bottles believed to contain liquid MDMA," he said, adding that the total value of the drugs seized was estimated at RM152,400.
Hussein said the syndicate was believed to have been active since February and had been using Malaysia as a base to process and package drugs before attempting to smuggle them overseas.
He said the syndicate was also believed to have made several overseas shipments before it was crippled in an operation launched on July 1.
Hussein said the syndicate deliberately operated from remote locations, including village houses, to process the drugs before transporting them by sea to Indonesia.
"They established their operational base in the Klang Valley, making it easier to use sea routes to send the drugs to neighbouring countries.
"They used remote areas as operational bases to deceive the authorities and avoid detection," he said.
Initial urine screening found that three of the eight suspects tested positive for methamphetamine.
Checks also found that none of the suspects had previous criminal records. All eight were remanded for 11 days from July 1 to July 11 to assist investigations.
Hussein said police were still investigating the syndicate's wider network, including identifying the recipients of the drug shipments overseas.
"This is still under investigation. We will continue working with our counterpart narcotics agencies in the relevant countries to determine who received the drugs and identify other members of the syndicate," he said.
He also revealed that most of the suspects did not possess valid immigration documents.
"Three had valid documents, while five did not. At this stage, we are unable to determine when they entered Malaysia.
"However, we believe they entered the country through illegal routes," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 for drug trafficking, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment, and not fewer than 12 strokes of the cane upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Death calculator: DOSM's new tool reveals top health risks by demographic
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Statistics Department's (DOSM) latest interactive calculator is giving Malaysians an insight into the leading causes of death among people of similar age and gender.
While it does not predict when or how an individual will die, the online tool estimates the most likely causes of death based on historical mortality data.
Available at
https://kalkulator.dosm.gov.my/cod/
, users enter basic details such as their age, gender and demographic profile before the calculator generates the diseases or conditions that account for the highest number of deaths among people with similar characteristics.
The estimates are based on mortality data collected between 2000 and 2023.
Rather than predicting an individual's fate, the calculator highlights the health risks commonly faced by people within the same demographic group.
For example, ischaemic heart disease may emerge as the leading cause of death among men approaching their 40s, while pneumonia may rank highest among younger women.
The initiative reflects DOSM's efforts to make official statistics more accessible and easier for the public to understand through interactive digital tools.
The calculator has also attracted attention on social media, with many users describing it as an engaging way to present public health data and encourage greater awareness of personal health risks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang police to intensify crackdown as World Cup enters final stages
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang police will intensify operations against illegal betting as the FIFA World Cup enters its final stages, with 31 people arrested statewide so far.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the arrests were made during raids on 26 premises, adding that six of those detained were Chinese nationals.
"As the tournament progresses into the final stages, we will further intensify our operations. The operations will continue," he told reporters after witnessing the disposal of case exhibits at the northeast district police headquarters here today.
Police would release a comprehensive report on the operation once it concluded.
Lim said illegal betting syndicates no longer operated from specific hotspots, as they could conduct activities from apartments, houses, shops and other premises.
"There are no specific hotspots anymore. Illegal betting can take place anywhere.
"We need information from the public to identify the main betting centres. While we also take action against smaller operators, our priority is to cripple the syndicates' main operations," he added.
Before this, Penang police had arrested 28 people and dismantled two football betting call centres under Op Soga XI, targeting illegal gambling during the FIFA World Cup 2026.
The
operation
would run until July 19 to curb football betting activities linked to the tournament.
Earlier, police disposed of seized case exhibits worth RM1.276 million after obtaining court orders.
The disposal involved 565 investigation papers, comprising 218 criminal investigation papers and 347 commercial crime investigation papers, all from the northeast district.
Among the items destroyed were 1,722 cartons of contraband cigarettes, 1,913 bottles of liquor, 25 entertainment equipment units, 726 gambling machines, and 173,000 pirated VCDs and DVDs.
Lim said the court had approved the disposal to prevent exhibits from accumulating in police store facilities.
He also thanked the Attorney-General's Chambers and the court for facilitating the process.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chegubard disqualified from Negri Sembilan polls after RM5,000 sedition fine
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Political activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, has been disqualified from contesting in the Negri Sembilan state election after the Sessions Court here today fined him RM5,000 for publishing seditious content.
His lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, said the fine rendered Chegubard ineligible to stand as an election candidate under Article 48 of the Federal Constitution.
He said the defence respected the court's decision.
"We appealed for Chegubard to be spared a custodial sentence, and Alhamdulillah, the court did not impose one.
"At the same time, we also requested that the fine not exceed RM2,000 because under Article 48 of the Federal Constitution, any individual fined RM2,000 or more is disqualified from contesting in elections.
"We had also informed the court that on July 18, Chegubard had been entrusted by Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan), which has recently been admitted into Perikatan Nasional (PN), to contest in the Negri Sembilan state election.
"However, with today's RM5,000 fine, he is no longer eligible to contest. That is the court's decision, and we accept and respect it," he told reporters after the proceedings.
Muhammad Rafique said the defence would file an appeal against the Sessions Court's ruling in the near future.
"I believe we have strong grounds for an appeal, and we hope Malaysians will pray for Chegubard's success in the High Court," he said.
Earlier, Sessions Court judge Rasidah Roslee found the 48-year-old guilty of publishing seditious material two years ago and imposed a RM5,000 fine after ruling that the defence had failed to raise a reasonable doubt at the close of its case.
The court also ordered Chegubard to serve 20 months' imprisonment should he fail to pay the fine.
According to the charge sheet, Chegubard was accused of publishing seditious content on social media at about 6.30pm on April 26.
He was charged under Section 4(1)(c) of the Sedition Act 1948, which provides for a fine of up to RM5,000, imprisonment of up to three years, or both, upon conviction.
Nomination day for the 16th Negri Sembilan state election has been set for July 18, with polling scheduled for Aug 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Private college employee charged with sexual assault, sodomy of two teens
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A private college employee pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to three counts of committing physical sexual assault and carnal intercourse against nature against two teenage boys.
Fikri Zainal Abidin, 38, claimed trial after the charges were read before judge Awang Kerisnada Awang Mahmud.
According to the charges, the accused committed physical sexual assault against two 15-year-old boys and intentionally committed carnal intercourse against the order of nature against one of them.
The offences were allegedly committed at a house in Seksyen 18 here at 11pm on June 20 and June 23, and at a college hostel in Seksyen U1 at 9.51am on June 29.
For the physical sexual assault, the accused was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum 20-year prison sentence and whipping upon conviction.
The charge of carnal intercourse against the order of nature falls under Section 377C of the Penal Code, which provides for a prison term of between five and 20 years, and whipping.
Deputy public prosecutors Roshaira Rosly and KS Rinisha Priya did not offer bail.
Fikri, who was unrepresented, pleaded for bail, saying he needed to appoint a lawyer and care for his parents.
However, Awang Kerisnada advised the accused that he could appoint counsel while in detention.
The court fixed Aug 11 for mention.
It was previously reported that police arrested a man in his 30s following reports lodged by two 15-year-old students at a college in Glenmarie regarding sodomy and molestation.
Investigations revealed the first victim claimed he was molested in the hostel toilet, while the second victim claimed he was sodomised at the suspect's house in Seksyen 18.
The suspect has a
prior criminal record
for sexual offences against children, police said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farmers' group warns against making growers easy targets under new rice bills
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tougher laws governing the padi and rice sector must not make farmers easy enforcement targets while powerful industry players escape scrutiny, a farmers' group warned.
Malaysian Padi Farmers Brotherhood Organisation (Pesawah) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said stronger action was needed to curb smuggling, supply hoarding, grade falsification, and market manipulation, which had long harmed farmers and consumers.
However, he said enforcement must be fair and cover the entire supply chain, including millers, wholesalers, importers, and retailers.
"We must not miss the forest for the trees.
"Farmers must not become the easiest group to penalise when they are the weakest players in the industry's supply chain," he told the New Straits Times today.
Rashid was responding to the tabling of the Control of Padi and Rice (Amendment) Bill 2026 yesterday, which seeks to impose heavier penalties and expand regulatory powers over the sector.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1484516/new-bill-seeks-tougher-controls-padi-and-rice-sector
The Bill also sought to expand the Agriculture and Food Security Minister's powers to introduce more comprehensive regulations covering the licensing of padi buyers, the grading and pricing of rice, as well as controls on the physical movement of padi and rice to curb hoarding and smuggling.
Rashid said any expansion of the minister's powers must be accompanied by clear checks and balances.
He said new policies and regulations should improve transparency, efficiency and farmers' welfare instead of increasing their burden or restricting their ability to market their produce.
He also welcomed the proposed Regulatory Impact Analysis under the Malaysia Productivity Corporation and post-implementation review after two years, but said engagement with farmers must be genuine.
"Farmers' representatives must be taken seriously because they are the people most affected by every policy change in the padi and rice industry," he said.
He said the amendments should become a catalyst for wider reforms, including fair and reasonable padi prices, greater transparency in the supply chain and balanced protection for farmers and consumers.
Rashid also urged the government to review the long-standing issue of the controlled price of local white rice, which had remained at RM2.60 per kg, unchanged for 18 years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Netanyahu pushes controversial bills in final pre-election dash
Article:
JERUSALEM: With the Israeli parliament set to dissolve in the coming days, just four months before national elections, the government of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is racing to pass a series of bills, drawing fierce criticism from opposition.
Seven pieces of legislation are currently on the agenda of the Knesset, Israel's 120-seat parliament, each a priority for one or more parties in Netanyahu's ruling coalition.
By striking these political bargains, Netanyahu hopes to shore up his alliances and head into the late October election from a position of strength, despite opinion polls suggesting he might struggle to return to office.
The veteran prime minister, who has held office for nearly two decades across multiple terms, is facing public anger over a series of failures – particularly those that led to Hamas's unprecedented Oct 7, 2023 attack, which triggered the war in Gaza.
"While they're busy with their coalition's survival, we're fighting for the country and for the interests of Israel's citizens," opposition leader Yair Lapid wrote on X.
The bill that has generated the most controversy concerns exemptions from military service for students engaged in religious studies – a longstanding demand of the ultra-Orthodox parties, which have repeatedly threatened in the past to bring down the government if their constituents were not granted such exemptions.
The bill, approved in a preliminary reading in June, states that "the State of Israel shall recognise individuals who commit themselves to long-term Torah study as performing meaningful service for the benefit of the State and the Jewish people."
For months, the proposed "Basic Law: Torah Study" has brought thousands of Israelis onto the streets and inflamed debate on political television programmes.
"The government is exploiting the final days of the Knesset's session to pass laws against the army," Gadi Eisenkot, former military chief who has emerged as Netanyahu's main rival in the upcoming election, wrote on X.
He argued that only his newly formed party, Yashar (Straight) party, would be capable of forming the next government.
- LEGISLATIVE 'MARATHON' -
According to Eisenkot, the legislative "marathon" is aimed solely at passing laws that would no longer be possible once he comes to power.
In exchange for supporting the military exemption bill, lawmakers from the two ultra-Orthodox parties are expected to back legislation proposed by Netanyahu's Likud party, including a reform of the broadcasting sector aimed at overhauling the entire regulatory framework governing television, radio and streaming platforms.
Presented by its supporters as a measure designed to open the market to competition, the reform has been condemned by critics as an attempt to exert political control over the media, undermine the independence of news organisations and provide economic benefits to outlets considered close to the government.
Another bill under consideration aims to reform the status of the state attorney general, who currently also serves as the government's legal adviser.
Netanyahu's Likud party seeks to weaken the position, currently held by his vocal critic Gali Baharav-Miara, by stipulating that the legal adviser's opinions would no longer be binding on the government.
Here too, the opposition accuses the government of trying to eliminate an independent check on its power and to politicise criminal prosecutions.
Finally, at the urging of the religious parties, the government is seeking to repeal a reform introduced by the previous administration that ended the religious authority's monopoly over issuing kosher certification by opening the sector to competition.
Once again, critics say the bill appears tailored to the demands of the ultra-Orthodox parties, particularly Shas, which has pushed for it in order to protect the religious institution's interests at the expense of consumers.
The original kosher reform was expected to reduce costs for restaurant owners.
Additional bills are still being debated in parliamentary committees, but heated disagreements – including within the governing coalition itself – make it difficult for all of the measures to pass before the end of the legislative term.
The Knesset is due to dissolve automatically on July 15, marking the end of its summer session, with parliamentary elections scheduled for no later than October 27.
However, in order to complete ongoing legislative proceedings, lawmakers can extend the session by up to 12 days, giving Netanyahu and his allies additional time to secure passage of the bills.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao road to boost border economy, says Anwar
Article:
hifzuddin@bh.com.my
Pix by Wan Nabil Nasir
BUKIT KAYU HITAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is confident that the opening of the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam border complex with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex will boost the economic potential between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly in the border regions.
He said the new route is expected to resolve the traffic congestion previously experienced at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complex and the Sadao CIQ complex.
According to Anwar, the route will expedite travel for container trucks carrying goods and ensure smoother movement for tourists between the two countries.
"Today marks another historic moment, particularly for northern Malaysia and southern Thailand, through this joint venture project that will stimulate economic development in both regions.
"We have gone beyond normal diplomatic relations by demonstrating our commitment to establishing a Special Border Economic Zone covering northern Malaysia and southern Thailand to spur economic growth," he said.
Anwar said this at the opening and operationalisation ceremony of the new road today.
Also present were Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, and Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail.
Anwar said developing the border area must be a joint effort between Malaysia and Thailand to ensure regional prosperity is felt by the local population while guaranteeing regional stability.
"This is not something that can be implemented quickly or easily, but I believe that with the determination of the leadership of Thailand and Malaysia, we can manage this new development well for the people.
"Apart from southern Thailand, the benefits will also be received by states like Kedah, Perlis, Kelantan, and Penang, which produce various agricultural and manufactured products exported through this route," he said.
Meanwhile, Anutin said the opening of the new road will ease cross-border travel and cargo movement while reducing congestion at the Sadao CIQ, the busiest gateway on the Malaysia-Thailand border.
He said Thailand is ready to discuss further cooperation involving its provinces of Satun, Songkhla, and Narathiwat, which hold significant potential for future growth.
"Moving forward, we will see various goods from Malaysia delivered overland to Thailand and onward to China or as far as Russia more smoothly. Similarly, goods from Thailand can easily be sent to Malaysia, straight to Singapore, and up to Indonesia. This will open up various job and business opportunities that benefit both countries," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia says landfill fire near Jakarta extinguished
Article:
JAKARTA: A landfill fire on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital, Jakarta, has been put out after burning for more than a week as evacuees returned home, a disaster agency spokesman said on Friday.
The Jatiwaringin landfill in the Tangerang district west of Jakarta caught fire on June 30, prompting the local government to declare a two-week emergency.
More than 230 people earlier evacuated the area and 331 cases of acute respiratory syndrome were reported, with no hospitalisations, according to the local disaster mitigation agency.
The fire – which burned an area at the landfill measuring around 15 hectares (37 acres) – had been completely extinguished by Thursday evening, National Disaster Mitigation Agency spokesman Abdul Muhari said in a statement.
Firefighters were carrying out an "intensive" cooling-down phase to douse the site, he added.
"The joint team... remains on full alert to accelerate total wetting" of the burned area, Abdul said, adding that all of the evacuees had returned to their homes.
The haze from the fire as well as the water bombing by helicopters did not disrupt operations at the nearby Soekarno-Hatta International Airport.
Environment ministry official Rizal Irawan said last week that authorities will investigate the cause of the fire, according to local media.
Indonesia's Meteorology, Climatology, and Geophysics Agency (BMKG) warned that the country's dry season will be more intense and prolonged than average – in part due to the El Nino weather pattern heightening risks of wildfires and droughts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asean to discuss engagement with Myanmar at special meeting, Philippines says
Article:
MANILA:  An upcoming meeting between foreign ministers of the Southeast Asian grouping Asean and their counterpart from Myanmar will tackle the country's civil war and its engagement with the bloc after five years in the cold, the Philippines said on Friday.
As chair of the 11-member Association of Southeast Asian Nations, the Philippines will lead Sunday's gathering in Bangkok, the first in-person meeting with Myanmar's top diplomat since a 2021 coup and ensuing conflict that led to the exclusion of the ruling generals from the bloc's summits.
The coup by an army that has ruled Myanmar for five of the past six decades triggered chaos, with a crackdown on protests leading to a civil war in which an estimated 100,000 people have been killed and millions displaced, with the military accused of widespread atrocities. Myanmar's military denies those allegations. The country is now ruled by a nominally civilian government following an election earlier this year, with the former armed forces commander and junta leader Min Aung Hlaing as president.
The Philippine foreign ministry stressed Myanmar remained an integral part of Asean and Sunday's meeting would be informal, giving Myanmar's foreign minister a chance to brief counterparts on the situation in the country.
"They are expected to exchange views on Asean's engagement with Myanmar, as well as on possible concrete steps in which Myanmar may address concerns on the cessation of violence, constructive dialogue among concerned parties, and humanitarian assistance," it said in a statement.
MYANMAR PUSHBACK ON PEACE PLAN
Min Aung Hlaing is seeking to end the impasse with Asean and made his first state visit to an Asean member country last week.
Central to Myanmar's exclusion was his failure to implement a "five-point consensus" he agreed with Asean after the coup, which outlined steps towards de-escalation and dialogue between warring groups.
But normalising ties could be made more challenging by a motion in Myanmar's military-aligned parliament to counter the peace plan, calling it interference in its internal affairs and a violation of Asean's core principles.
An article on Friday that stretched across two pages of the Global New Light of Myanmar, the military's mouthpiece, said lawmakers had endorsed a resolution asking the government to review and challenge Asean's position.
"During the discussions, lawmakers from both houses largely supported the motion, arguing that Asean should reassess its position on Myanmar following political developments and the formation of a new elected government," the newspaper said.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke orders action against all helmetless campaigners
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has been ordered to issue summonses to anyone found riding motorcycles without helmets while campaigning in the Johor election.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the department must not practise double standards when enforcing the law, including against members of parties in the government.
"My stand has always been clear and consistent.
"When I raised a similar issue involving Barisan Nasional leaders during the Slim by-election campaign nearly six years ago, I stressed that there was nothing wrong with riding motorcycles while campaigning.
"However, every rider must wear a helmet and set a good example for the public," he said in a statement today.
Loke said the principle applied to everyone without exception, including PH candidates and campaign workers.
"Road safety laws do not recognise political parties.
"I have instructed the RTD to issue summonses to anyone found violating the rules based on evidence from the viral video, regardless of their political background," he said.
Yesterday, a video showing a group of DAP campaign workers taking part in a PH motorcycle convoy, with several allegedly riding without helmets, went viral.
Social media users subsequently urged Loke to take action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar, Anutin launch new road linking Bukit Kayu Hitam and Sadao border complexes
Article:
BUKIT KAYU HITAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his Thai counterpart, Anutin Charnvirakul, today launched the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam border complex with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex.
The Home Ministry said the launch reflects the strong commitment of both countries to strengthening diplomatic ties, enhancing cross-border strategic cooperation, and improving regional connectivity.
The new road will open to all road users at 6am tomorrow, while the existing border crossing linking Bukit Kayu Hitam and Danok will be permanently closed from midnight tomorrow.
"The opening of the new road marks a strategic milestone that reflects the success of close cooperation between Malaysia and Thailand in developing a more modern, integrated and resilient border infrastructure," the ministry said.
It said the new infrastructure also symbolises the longstanding friendship between Malaysia and Thailand while supporting the Asean Connectivity agenda by enhancing the movement of people, trade, investment, and tourism across the region.
The ministry added that the project is expected to deliver wide-ranging benefits, including strengthening bilateral strategic ties through closer cross-border cooperation and improving connectivity along the Asean North-South Economic Corridor.
It is also expected to facilitate trade, logistics, and supply chains, stimulate economic growth in border areas, improve cross-border travel efficiency, and strengthen border security through modern infrastructure and integrated control systems, the statement read.
The ministry expressed confidence that the new road would usher in a new era of Malaysia-Thailand cooperation, support cross-border economic growth, and enhance the management of entry points through safer, more efficient, and more transparent operations.
It also thanked all ministries, departments, enforcement agencies, and strategic partners in both countries that contributed to the completion of the high-impact project.
In line with the aspirations of Malaysia Madani, the ministry said it would continue strengthening border management through modern infrastructure, technology adoption, and international strategic cooperation to safeguard national security.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police smash synthetic drug syndicate supplying Klang Valley, Kuantan clubs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a synthetic drug syndicate supplying entertainment outlets in the Klang Valley and Kuantan, seizing RM13.2 million worth of drugs in a series of raids.
Federal police Narcotics Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said seven syndicate members, comprising six local men and a foreign woman aged between 25 and 35, were arrested in Op Musca on July 3.
He said the operation involved five raids around the Klang Valley and Pahang, targeting condominium residential units that were used as locations to store and package drugs.
"In the same raids, police also seized various types of drugs, including 84.3kg of Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) powder, 4.4kg of ecstasy pills, 2.4kg of ketamine and 730g of Erimin 5 pills.
"The total value of the drugs seized is estimated at RM13.2 million. The syndicate is believed to have been active since January.
"Investigations also found that the syndicate used condominium units as storage and packaging locations before distributing the drugs to prevent their activities from being detected by the authorities," he said at a press conference at the Kuala Lumpur police headquarters.
Hussein said the drugs stored at the condominiums would be handed over to drug distributors at the condominium parking areas before being transported for distribution to entertainment outlets.
He said apart from the drug seizure, police also confiscated various assets worth RM1.28 million, including 10 vehicles believed to be linked to the syndicate's activities.
"Urine screening tests found that four of the seven suspects tested positive for methamphetamine.
"Checks also found that only one of them had a previous criminal record, involving a gambling offence," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 for drug trafficking, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment and not fewer than 12 strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Landslide kills five in Philippines as biggest typhoon in decades nears Taiwan
Article:
KEELUNG: Five people were killed in a landslide in the Philippines, and hundreds in Taiwan were evacuated from their homes, as the biggest typhoon in decades swept towards the region on Friday.
Typhoon Bavi is expected to pound Taiwan's north and east, as well as Japan's remote southwestern islands, on Friday and Saturday before smashing into China, which has been hit by deadly storms this week.
A landslide triggered by heavy rains enhanced by Bavi killed at least five people and left six others missing on the Philippines' southern island of Mindanao, police said.
Locals in Taiwan's port city of Keelung, which is expected to be one of the hardest hit areas on the island, stocked up on food, taped windows, and stacked sandbags along shop fronts, heeding warnings from authorities to take precautions.
"They're saying it's going to be huge; of course that's scary, right?" Keelung grocery store owner Chang Shih-huo, 76, told AFP.
"We've stocked up on some instant noodles and bread and stuff like that. Once the wind and rain really start picking up, we'll have to close the shop."
After hitting Guam and the Northern Marianas on Monday as a super typhoon, Bavi was downgraded to a typhoon as it moved across the Pacific Ocean.
Bavi's maximum sustained wind speeds were 162km per hour, with gusts of around 198 kilometres per hour, on Friday – slower than on Thursday – the Central Weather Administration (CWA) said.
"The typhoon is likely to continue weakening because environmental conditions are not favourable," CWA forecaster Wang Ping-hsiang told AFP.
"The greatest impact is expected in Taipei, New Taipei, Keelung and Yilan, while the heaviest rainfall is forecast for mountainous areas in central and northern Taiwan."
Bavi's strong-wind radius of 380km  will make it the largest typhoon to hit Taiwan in more than 30 years.
Many schools and businesses were shuttered on Friday across northern and eastern Taiwan, including the capital Taipei, and hundreds of domestic and international flights were cancelled.
In Keelung, locals flocked to a fresh food market to buy fruit and vegetables, street vendors secured their stands, and a temple covered and tied down an outdoor statue ahead of the storm.
"What you're seeing now is the most remarkable sight we haven't seen in 10 years," Keelung restaurant owner Penny Pan, 48, said as her husband placed sandbags at the entrance to their eatery.
"In the past we never used sandbags to prepare for typhoons. But this time they're forecasting Force 10 gusts, so the captains and fishermen have all been saying we need to be better prepared," Pan said.
- 'STRONG WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN' -
Bavi is expected to dump up to a metre of rain, raising concerns about potential flooding and landslides.
More than a thousand people have been evacuated from their homes, mostly in the mountainous county of Hualien in Taiwan's east, where authorities are monitoring two barrier dams.
Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te urged people in areas likely to be most affected by Bavi to remain on "high alert."
More than 20,000 troops, along with machinery, equipment and vehicles, were on standby to respond to emergencies.
"We're worried," said Samuel Fu, who works in a noodle shop in the coastal district of Bali, in New Taipei City.
"This is the first time we've experienced such a big typhoon... since we opened the shop," 20-year-old Fu told AFP.
On Japan's remote Sakashima islands, schools and offices were closed in some areas as the typhoon approached, with forecasters warning of high waves, storms and landslides.
"We've covered objects with nets so that they won't fly away...the storm is getting gradually stronger right now," Masaru Nakamura, who works at a hotel on Miyako island, told AFP.
On Okinawa, Koki Ohama said his water sports and barbeque business in the major city of Naha would suffer from the storm.
"We were originally fully reserved this weekend, but bookings were all cancelled, so it's painful," the 30-year-old said.
After slamming into the Japanese islands and sweeping past Taiwan's northern tip, Bavi is expected to make landfall in eastern China over the weekend.
Extreme weather has already wreaked havoc on southern and central China this week, with storms leaving at least 39 dead and causing dozens of rivers to overflow and a reservoir dam to burst.
Oceans experienced their hottest June on record and could set fresh highs in the months ahead, the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service said last week.
Warmer oceans help tropical storms to intensify and add more moisture, which can fall as heavy rain.
Adding to the mix is the return this year of El Nino, a natural climate phenomenon that warms Pacific surface temperatures and typically occurs every two to seven years.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UAE's E& to sell Vodafone stake to French telecoms tycoon Niel for nearly US$6bil
Article:
DUBAI: UAE telecoms group E& said on Friday it would sell its stake in Vodafone for US$5.95 billion to the family group of French billionaire Xavier Niel, ending its investment in the British firm after a review of its international portfolio.
The deal prices Vodafone shares at 112.5 pence each, a 15 per cent premium to the last close of 97.76 pence, and will result in a net cash return of about US$1.3 billion, E& said.
The Abu Dhabi-based company said its decision to exit Vodafone reflected the "natural evolution" of its priorities to "sharpen its strategic focus on core businesses" while unlocking cash from the exit.
Niel is the founder and owner of French telecoms firm Iliad. Iliad Group did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.
E& said the shares would be sold simultaneously through off-market block trades to three financial institutions, which would hold them until the Niel family group's acquisition vehicle completed regulatory requirements.
It also said it would no longer seek to influence Vodafone's board or management, and that its board representative had stepped down as a non-executive director.
There was no immediate statement from Vodafone on the stake sale.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan police, MCBA strengthen cooperation at border complexes
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan police will continue working closely with the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) following the agency's takeover of operations at two more Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes in the state.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said MCBA assumed responsibility for the Pengkalan Kubor and Bukit Bunga ICQS complexes earlier this month.
He said the agency's role is focused on security and enforcement within the ICQS complexes, covering legal entry and exit points along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
"Police will continue to cooperate with MCBA as there may be attempts to smuggle illegal goods through these authorised routes.
"We will work together whenever necessary to ensure border security is maintained," he said.
Yusoff said MCBA's jurisdiction is confined to the ICQS complexes, while police remain responsible for monitoring surrounding areas, including illegal crossings frequently used by smugglers.
"MCBA oversees the ICQS complexes and official border crossings, while police continue enforcement operations outside these areas, including illegal jetties along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
"If there are attempts to bring in or take out prohibited items, we will work together with MCBA," he said.
He added that Kelantan police stand ready to assist the agency in strengthening border security and enforcement.
The move followed MCBA's takeover of the
Rantau Panjang ICQS Complex on Jan 1, 2025
, as part of the agency's pilot operation to assume control of selected national border entry points in phases.
Rantau Panjang was chosen as the first operational site due to its strategic importance as a key land gateway between Malaysia and Thailand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mais: Friday prayers at IOI City Mall allowed temporarily since Sept 2024
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Temporary permission for Friday prayers at Musolla IOI City Mall in Putrajaya has been in effect since Sept 6, 2024, following consent from the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah.
Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) chairman Senator Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said the approval was granted by Mais through the Selangor State Mosque and Surau Governance Committee.
He said the decision was made following assessments that found the premises housed a large number of Muslim male staff and workers, while also receiving many Muslim visitors.
"They faced difficulties in performing Friday prayers as the nearest mosques were located far from the premises. The nearest mosques to the premises are Masjid Al-Mustaqim Kampung Dato' Abu Bakar Baginda, about 7.6km away, and Masjid Uniten in Kajang, about 7.7km away.
"Furthermore, both mosques were unable to accommodate the large number of congregants attending Friday prayers," he said in a statement today.
Salehuddin reiterated that the permission granted for Friday prayers at Musolla IOI City Mall is temporary.
"The permission will cease once a new mosque near the premises has been built and can meet the needs of the congregation, including employees at the premises, to perform Friday prayers."
He said Mais, together with the Selangor Islamic Religious Department, would continue to ensure that the management and implementation of Friday prayers in the state were carried out in an orderly manner, in accordance with Islamic law and relevant legal provisions, for the benefit and welfare of the Muslim community.
On Wednesday, it was reported that the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, will not consent to proposals for Friday prayers to be held at shopping malls in the state.
Salehuddin said the establishment of mosques and suraus, or the use of any building for such purposes in the state, required written permission from Mais.
He said
Mais had been informed that Sultan Sharafuddin
, as the head of Islam in the state, would not grant permission for the proposal.
Salehuddin said the Sultan's decision was in line with Islamic teachings to strengthen the role of mosques as primary places of worship, knowledge dissemination and dakwah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GFM Services bags RM148.2mil PIC turnaround work contracts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: GFM Services Bhd's unit HIMS Integrated Services Sdn Bhd has secured RM148.2 million worth of two release orders for major turnaround works at the Pengerang Integrated Complex (PIC) in Johor.
In a statement, the integrated facilities management services provider said the orders were awarded by Pengerang Petrochemical Company Sdn Bhd and Pengerang Refining Company Sdn Bhd under the existing Integrated Turnaround Main Mechanical and Maintenance Mechanical Static (TA4MS) contract.
The works are related to the first major plant turnaround at the complex since the integrated downstream complex began operations in 2019.
The project is expected to involve a substantial volume of manhours, reflecting the scale and complexity of the maintenance activities.
It is expected to contribute positively to GFM's earnings for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2027.
Group managing director Ruslan Nordin said the award offers an opportunity for the company's energy division to showcase its capabilities in executing large-scale turnaround projects within a highly regulated downstream environment.
"With extensive works to be carried out within a single integrated complex, this turnaround is expected to be among the largest of its kind in Malaysia.
"It will also provide meaningful revenue visibility for GFM's energy division," he said.
The company's energy division comprises Highbase Strategic Sdn Bhd, HIMS Integrated Services Sdn Bhd — a 51 per cent-owned joint venture with Singapore-based oil and gas engineering company Mun Siong Engineering Limited — and Shapadu CR Asia Sdn Bhd.
For the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, the division generated RM51.4 million in revenue, a 62.2 per cent of GFM's total revenue, underscoring its growing contribution to its business.
All three companies are resident contractors at PIC under the TA4MS framework, which covers specialised mechanical maintenance, inspection, repair and replacement of static plant equipment during planned shutdowns and turnaround periods.
Ruslan said GFM has transformed from a pure-play facilities management provider into a broader services group with an expanding energy business.
"Through strategic acquisitions, we have built a stronger platform, deepened our technical capabilities and expanded our access to large-scale downstream maintenance opportunities," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lianson Fleet buys Ultramax-class vessels for over RM213mil to expand dry bulk shipping ops
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lianson Fleet Group Bhd plans to buy two Ultramax-class bulk carriers for US$52.32 million (RM213.2 million) as the company expands beyond its offshore support vessel business and strengthens its dry bulk shipping operations.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Lianson Fleet said its Singapore-based unit Lianson Fleet Pte Ltd (LFPL) had signed two separate memorandum of agreements to buy MV Tian Mu Shan and MV Yan Dang Shan from unrelated companies incorporated in China.
The acquisition will be funded through a combination of internally generated funds and bank borrowings.
The company is currently in discussions with lenders to part-finance the purchase to optimise its capital structure while preserving financial flexibility.
Lianson Fleet said the vessels would be its first Ultramax-class bulk carriers, marking a step up from its existing Supramax bulk carrier fleet.
"The acquisition is in line with the company's strategy to expand its marine transportation fleet and diversify beyond the offshore support vessels business segment.
"The vessels will strengthen the recurring income stream with its long-term charter potential and utilisation, thereby enhancing earnings visibility," it said.
Lianson Fleet added that demand and charter rates for bulk carriers remain robust, supported by regional trade flows of bulk commodities and longer shipping routes caused by trade disruptions, canal constraints and regional supply-demand imbalances.
Both vessels are registered under the Marshall Islands flag and have deadweight capacities of about 63,000 tonnes.
Under the agreements, Lianson Fleet will pay a 10 per cent deposit within three banking days after signing and escrow confirmation, with the balance to be deposited one banking day after receiving the sellers' notice of readiness.
Delivery of MV Tian Mu Shan is expected between June 11 and Aug 11, 2026, while MV Yan Dang Shan is scheduled for delivery between Sept 10 and Oct 10, 2026.
The company expects the acquisition to be completed by October 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perkeso's officers not involved in alleged claims fraud, says CEO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has reassured the public that its officers were not involved in the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive fraud claims investigated by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
Perkeso group chief executive Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the alleged fraud involved falsified documents submitted by claimants, which initially appeared genuine before being detected through its artificial intelligence (AI)-powered anti-fraud system.
Mohammed Azman noted that the system detected discrepancies in claims as early as December 2024, prompting an internal investigation before the findings were handed over to the MACC.
"The officers who processed the claims were not involved in the fraud. They acted based on documents submitted to them, which at the time appeared to be genuine," he told reporters at a press conference.
More to come
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bickering during election campaigns reflects mature politics, says Ismail Sabri
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Criticisms traded between parties forming the federal government during the Johor election campaign are normal and reflect political maturity, says Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob.
The former prime minister said differences in stance during a campaign should not be viewed as unusual because the parties involved continue to discharge their duties professionally.
He said this situation was previously experienced when he led the government, which comprised Umno, Pas, Bersatu, and several other parties.
"That is normal; it has happened before. During the Melaka election, for example, we differed.
"On Tuesday nights, we hit out at each other. But the next day at the cabinet meeting, it was business as usual. It means we are professional in that sense," he told reporters after a breakfast programme with Barisan Nasional candidate for the Pasir Raja state seat, Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba, at Taman Sri Saujana here today.
At the same time, the Bera member of parliament said Barisan Nasional appreciated the readiness of Pas to urge its supporters to back Barisan Nasional candidates in seats the party was not contesting in the Johor polls.
He said such cooperation was not foreign as it had been implemented previously under Muafakat Nasional.
"We thank them very much, if it is true. This is also not strange; previously, during Muafakat Nasional, Pas supported us openly in Selangor. We hope that if it is true they are helping us here, it might be the same in Negri Sembilan," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perkeso's Lindung 24 Jam opt-out available from Monday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The online opt-out facility for the Non-Employment Injury Scheme (Lindung 24 Jam) will be available from Monday, said the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso).
Its group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said contributors who choose to withdraw from the scheme will be required to complete and sign a liability waiver declaration.
Azman noted that this will serve as a legal safeguard in the event of future disputes, particularly involving accidents occurring outside working hours.
"Those who wish to opt out of the scheme must complete what we regard as a liability waiver declaration.
"If they choose not to continue with the scheme after signing the declaration, neither Perkeso nor the employer will be responsible. They should not blame us or their employer in the future," he said at a press conference today.
More to come
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Election Commission targets 70 per cent voter turnout for Johor polls
Article:
PONTIAN: The Election Commission (EC) expects a 70 per cent voter turnout, translating to 1.8 million voters, for the 16th Johor election tomorrow.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the anticipated increase in turnout compared to the 2022 state election was driven by growing public interest in the state's democratic process.
"We see a relatively high tendency and interest to vote this time, alongside continuous campaigning.
"We expect the voter turnout could reach around 70 per cent, as the spirit among the people to be involved in the democratic process has been quite high lately," he said after inspecting ballot boxes at the Permas Multipurpose Hall in Kukup today.
Johor has more than 2.7 million registered voters, with 1,076 polling centres involving 4,849 streams.
Ramlan said the ballot box checking process was critical to ensure all equipment to be used tomorrow is complete and in order.
"So far, I understand that almost all returning officers in the 56 centres across Johor have completed this. Alhamdullilah, the process went very smoothly. Voting tomorrow will begin at 8am, while most polling centres will close at 5pm," he said.
He also reminded postal voters to return their ballot papers by 6pm tomorrow.
Ramlan said 24,677 postal ballot papers had been issued to election officials and citizens overseas, and all must be returned before voting closes.
A total of 43,036 EC personnel will be on duty to ensure the smooth running of the state election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia sharply higher at midday, tracking regional gains
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia was sharply higher at midday today, tracking gains in regional markets, as buying in financial heavyweights supported market sentiment.
At 12.30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) surged 17.19 points, or 1.03 per cent, to 1,694.83, from Thursday's close of 1,677.64.
The benchmark index opened 5.14 points higher at 1,682.78, and moved between 1,682.78 and 1,696.25 during the morning trading session.
Market breadth was positive with gainers outnumbering losers 570 to 358, while 516 counters were unchanged, 1,272 untraded, and 25 suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.64 billion units worth RM1.06 billion.
Regionally, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index grew 1.86  per cent to 24,476.69, South Korea's KOSPI Composite Index rose 5.49 per cent to 7,692.43, Japan's Nikkei 225 edged up 1.85 per cent to 68,999.33, while Singapore's Straits Times Index inched up 0.43 per cent to 5,457.96.
In a note today, Malacca Securities Bhd said that with the rebound in global markets and the slide in Brent oil prices, the market is immune to the United States (US)-Iran tensions.
"We expect the FBM KLCI to stay positive for the session. Markets may continue trading the technology sector alongside domestic plays with decent catalysts," it said.
Among heavyweights, Maybank gained eight sen to RM10.94, Public Bank rose six sen to RM4.91, Tenaga Nasional added two sen to RM14.32, CIMB was five sen higher at RM7.65, and IHH Healthcare advanced 17 sen to RM8.49.
As for the active stocks, Zetrix AI and HHRG were up one sen each to 73.5 sen and 10.5 sen, respectively, Nationgate increased seven sen to 87.5 sen, Tanco inched up half a sen to 19.5 sen, and SRKK AI climbed 1.5 sen to 62.5 sen.
Of the top gainers, Fraser & Neave added 76 sen to RM29.68, UMS Integration was 46 sen higher at RM8.22, Petronas Gas edged up 28 sen to RM17.76, Kuala Lumpur Kepong advanced 26 sen to RM21.48, and Nestle rose 22 sen to RM91.52.
Malaysian Pacific Industries led the losers list, falling 48 sen to RM45.78, while Vitrox shed 15 sen to RM7.39, SAM Engineering & Equipment slipped 13 sen to RM4.77, Allianz Malaysia lost 10 sen to RM21.02, and Apollo Food declined nine sen to RM5.88.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Index surged 102.50 points to 12,542.98, the FBMT 100 Index grew 104.54 points to 12,372.64, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index rose 106.55 points to 12,386.83.
The FBM 70 Index perked up 61.59 points to 17,859.23, while the FBM ACE Index dipped 7.53 points to 4,883.56.
Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index jumped 134.75 points to 19,962.83, the Plantation Index expanded 81.38 points to 9,158.36 and the Industrial Products and Services Index edged up 2.61 points to 183.27, while the Energy Index slid 0.60 of a point to 773.36.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SD Property, ESR expand Bukit Raja logistics hub
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd and its joint venture partner ESR are expanding the E-Metro Logistics Park in Bandar Bukit Raja with two new Grade A logistics facilities.
This adds more than 2.2 million square feet of warehouse space amid rising demand for modern logistics infrastructure.
The completion of Metrohub 4 and the groundbreaking of Metrohub 3 will lift the park's total logistics portfolio to more than 4.2 million sq ft, reinforcing its position as one of Malaysia's key industrial and logistics hubs.
Strategically located near Port Klang and linked to major Klang Valley expressways, the park caters to multinational companies serving regional and global markets.
The expansion follows the full occupancy of Metrohub 1 and Metrohub 2, which are leased to tenants from the e-commerce, third-party logistics and supply chain sectors.
Metrohub 4, spanning about 1.38 million sq ft, will be the largest ready-built multi-tenanted warehouse in the development.
Global beverage chain Mixue has secured 13.4 per cent of the facility's net lettable area for its regional distribution centre ahead of its opening.
Metrohub 3, scheduled for completion in the third quarter of 2027, will comprise about 840,000 sq ft across two warehouse blocks, including specialised storage for dangerous goods to meet growing demand driven by imports of electric vehicle components such as lithium batteries.
Group managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Azmir Merican said E-Metro Logistics Park is a key component of Bandar Bukit Raja's industrial ecosystem.
"Upon completion of both Metrohubs, the portfolio size within E-Metro Logistics Park will exceed 4.2 million sq ft of modern warehouse space, advancing our portfolio towards high-specification, institutional-grade logistics facilities targeted for multinational tenants."
ESR head of Southeast Asia Jai Mirpuri said Malaysia remains one of the region's most attractive logistics investment destinations.
"Demand for high-quality logistics infrastructure continues to grow across Southeast Asia, and Malaysia is one of the region's most compelling markets for that investment."
ESR Malaysia head David Aboud said the full occupancy of the first two phases and early leasing commitments at Metrohub 4 underscore strong customer demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Customs busts RM513,000 drug smuggling bids via courier parcels
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Selangor Customs Department foiled three separate attempts to smuggle various types of drugs worth RM513,045 through courier services in April and June.
State Customs director Mohamad Azhar Ahmad Paharazi said the first case involved the seizure of 781g of cannabis buds worth RM62,480 during an inspection of an imported courier box at a mail centre in Sepang on April 14.
"To evade detection, the drugs from a country in the Americas were declared as beeswax products for making candles, body care items, skin products, soap, and lotion. The recipient's address was located around Kuala Lumpur," he said in a statement today.
He said the second case on April 24 involved the seizure of 8,965g of syabu and heroin worth RM448,250 during an inspection of two export wooden pallet boxes under a single air waybill at a courier mail centre in Sepang.
Inspectors discovered 14 plastic packages containing suspected syabu and heroin hidden beneath the wooden pallets.
"The consignment, believed to have originated from Kuala Lumpur and bound for an Asian country, was declared as ceramic wine jars and porcelain vases to deceive the authorities," he said.
Azhar said the third case involved the seizure of 100 Erimin 5 pills worth RM2,000 during an inspection of a domestic courier parcel at a mail centre in Subang Jaya on June 9.
The drugs were found in blister packs placed inside a package of dry pet food, which was declared as kibble to mislead the authorities.
"Checks revealed the parcel was sent from Kuala Lumpur and was bound for Kota Kinabalu, Sabah," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI emerges as manufacturers' answer to rising costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: As manufacturers contend with rising production costs, supply chain disruptions, labour shortages, geopolitical uncertainties and increasingly demanding customer expectations, many are turning to intelligent, AI-powered solutions to improve resilience, productivity and operational efficiency.
Technology providers like HashMicro are responding to these challenges by expanding their manufacturing platforms with AI-native capabilities, enabling businesses to automate complex operations, optimise resources and make faster, data-driven decisions in an increasingly volatile market.
The company on Thursday introduced HashMicro X, an upgraded manufacturing solution designed to help businesses automate complex production processes, accelerate operational decision-making and optimise factory performance through artificial intelligence.
The launch comes as manufacturers move beyond conventional digitalisation to address mounting operational pressures.
While traditional enterprise software has enabled companies to standardise processes and centralise data, many systems still rely on users to interpret information, identify bottlenecks and determine corrective actions, limiting their ability to respond quickly to fast-changing business conditions.
"Manufacturers generate enormous amounts of operational data every day, but data alone doesn't improve productivity," said Nur Fadiyah Syifa, HashMicro's vice president of strategic communications.
"Businesses need AI that understands the context behind every order, machine, and production line, then recommends the next best action or takes routine actions automatically. That's the difference between software that records work and software that helps run the business," she said in a statement.
HashMicro X uses AI throughout the manufacturing process, allowing the software to examine operational data, suggest actions, and automate regular tasks instead of just keeping track of transactions and creating reports.
Nur Fadiyah said the technology is designed to help manufacturers detect operational anomalies earlier, automate workflows across production, inventory, procurement, maintenance and quality control, improve resource utilisation, and respond more effectively to disruptions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Husband, wife face multiple charges over alleged fire attacks
Article:
IPOH: A husband and wife were hit with six criminal charges in three separate courts here today, including three counts of committing mischief by fire.
M. Dhinakaran and B. Rahtheneswary, both 34, pleaded not guilty to three counts of committing mischief by fire in separate incidents last month before Sessions Court judge Ainul Shahrin Mohamad.
On the first charge, they were accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at a house in Laluan Rokam 13, Pekan Razaki, at 4am on June 4, which damaged the house and a car.
They were also accused of committing mischief by fire to destroy four houses in Taman Kledang Suria at 5am on June 11.
Both charges were framed under Section 436 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which provides for up to 20 years' imprisonment and a fine upon conviction.
On the third charge, they were accused of committing mischief by fire or using an explosive substance to damage the front door of a house in Menglembu Bestari at 3.30am on June 12.
The charge was framed under Section 435 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which carries a maximum jail term of 14 years and a fine upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor M. Raveenna prosecuted, while both accused were unrepresented. Raveenna proposed bail of RM50,000 each for all three charges.
Dhinakaran said he had no fixed income and had to support his mother and five children.
Rahtheneswary said she worked as a home tutor and supported her mother, who has asthma.
Ainul Shahrin granted bail of RM11,000 each and fixed Aug 11 for document submission, while ordering the pair to appoint legal counsel.
In separate proceedings before magistrate S. Punitha, Dhinakaran pleaded guilty to splashing red paint on a gate and a car at a house in Taman Restu Jaya at 5.20am on June 5.
The offence, under Section 427 of the Penal Code, carries imprisonment of between one and five years, a fine, or both.
Punitha fined him RM2,500, in default six months' imprisonment.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zulqarnain Wan Asmira prosecuted, while Dhinakaran was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK) lawyer Robert S. Gnanarajan.
In the same court, the couple were jointly charged with splashing red paint on a car parked at a house in Jalan Menglembu Bestari at 8.30am on June 8.
Dhinakaran pleaded guilty and was fined RM2,500, in default seven months' imprisonment. He was represented by YBGK lawyer Intan Shafini Mior Shahrin.
Rahtheneswary pleaded not guilty. Punitha granted her bail of RM2,000 and fixed Aug 19 for mention. She was represented by Robert.
In another magistrate's court, the couple were charged with committing mischief by causing damage to property at a house in Taman Temara at 5.30am on June 4.
Dhinakaran pleaded guilty before magistrate Norizan Ridzuan, while Rahtheneswary pleaded not guilty.
The charge, under Section 426 of the Penal Code read together with Section 34, carries up to five years' imprisonment, a fine, or both.
Norizan fined Dhinakaran RM2,500, in default seven months' imprisonment, while Rahtheneswary was granted bail of RM1,500 with one surety.
Deputy public prosecutor S. Nishaalini prosecuted, while Dhinakaran and Rahtheneswary were represented by YBGK lawyers Chan Hong Yan and E.P. Seeralasittan, respectively.
The court fixed Aug 12 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar touched by boy’s plantation journey to meet him in Segamat, gifts bicycle after visit
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared the heartwarming story of nine-year-old Muhammad Afif Ikhwan, who travelled through an oil palm plantation with his grandmother in the hope of meeting him.
He said the short encounter with the boy during a programme at Felda Palong Timur in Segamat had made a deep impression on him, prompting him to send someone to visit him and present him with a bicycle.
“What may appear to us as a small dream was, in fact, big enough to move his grandmother, Rosimah Mohammad, to ride a motorcycle through an oil palm plantation to fulfil her grandson’s wish.
“His little hand reached through the crowd, and we shook hands before bringing him onto the stage at Felda Palong Timur, Segamat, recently.
“I never expected that such a brief moment would carry such great meaning for Afif,” he said in a Facebook post today.
No matter how busy we are managing the affairs of the country and facing various major challenges, we must never lose our compassion in reaching out to others and showing humanity, he said.
The Prime Minister also hoped that the encounter would become a cherished memory that would inspire Muhammad Afif to continue his studies and contribute to his family, community and country in the future.
Yesterday, Bernama reported that Rosimah, 58, from Kampung Pudu, Segamat, rode a motorcycle along oil palm plantation tracks solely to give her grandson the opportunity to see the Prime Minister up close. — Bernama

Title: PM: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao road link to spur Malaysia-Thailand border economic growth
Article:
BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 10 — The new road alignment linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah and the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand will serve as a catalyst to propel economic growth in both regions, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said the project represented a historic milestone in Malaysia-Thailand relations and would strengthen cross-border connectivity, trade activities and economic cooperation between the two countries.
“Today marks another historical venture. And in this state, particularly the north of Peninsular Malaysia, we have to grapple with attempts to propel economic growth for the welfare of our people.
“And I believe a similar position in the south (of Thailand), and through this new venture project, we should be able to use it as a base to propel the economy of both regions,” he said at the launch of the new road alignment here today.
The ceremony was jointly officiated by Anwar and Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul.
Anwar said the initiative was part of broader efforts to establish a special economic border zone involving northern Malaysia and southern Thailand to spur economic growth and improve the livelihoods of communities in the border areas.
“We are extending it beyond normal diplomatic engagements, to having the resolve to try and create a special economic border zone. We cover the northern part of Malaysia and southern Thailand to propel economic growth, and it’s not something that can be done fast and easily,” he said.
He also noted that the proposed cooperation could spearhead new development opportunities for Perlis, Kedah, Kelantan, Perak and Penang, with strong commitment from the leadership of both countries.
Meanwhile, Anwar said Malaysia and Thailand had agreed to expedite the resolution of outstanding issues involving customs, immigration, fisheries and trade to facilitate businesses and traders from both countries.
“We have agreed to try and resolve it in the fastest manner possible. Because it would mean better lives, better livelihoods for our traders and business community,” he said.
Anwar also said Malaysia and Thailand remained committed to achieving the bilateral trade target of USD30 billion by 2027, adding that stronger cooperation at the border would help realise the goal.
He said both countries had demonstrated the ability to resolve long-standing issues through political commitment and close cooperation.
“Long-standing issues which have taken us years, sometimes decades, of negotiations, meetings after meetings. We tried to resolve most of it yesterday in a very speedy manner,” he said.
Anwar also expressed appreciation to Anutin for visiting the border area, saying such a move reflected the importance of understanding the needs of communities outside major cities.
Earlier, Anwar and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, welcomed the arrival of Anutin Charnvirakul and his wife Thananon Charnvirakul to the ceremony held at the Malaysia-Thailand border.
Anutin arrived in Malaysia yesterday for a two-day official visit at the invitation of Anwar. — Bernama

Title: Chegubard disqualified from Negeri Sembilan polls after RM5,000 fine in sedition case
Article:
PETALING JAYA, July 10 — Activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, can no longer contest in the upcoming Negeri Sembilan election following a RM5,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court today, after he was found guilty of publishing seditious material two years ago.
His lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, said the defence respected the court’s decision but would be filing an appeal soon,
Berita Harian
(BH) reported.
“However, with this RM5,000 fine, Chegubard is disqualified from contesting. That is the court’s decision and we accept and respect it,” he told reporters after proceedings.
Muhammad Rafique added: “We believe we have strong grounds for appeal and we hope for the prayers of all Malaysians for Chegubard’s success at the High Court.”
Earlier, Sessions Court judge Rasidah Roslee ruled that the defence had failed to raise reasonable doubt at the close of its case, and imposed the fine. The court also ordered Chegubard to serve 20 months in prison if he fails to pay, BH reported.
According to the charge sheet, Chegubard was accused of publishing a seditious post on social media at about 6.30pm on April 26, linking the Yang di Pertuan Agong Sultan Ibrahim, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, and two alleged gambling tycoons to a proposed casino project in Forest City.
The charge was framed under Section 4(1)(c) of the Sedition Act 1948, which carries a maximum penalty of RM5,000, up to three years’ imprisonment, or both, upon conviction.
Nomination day for the 16th Negeri Sembilan election is July 18, with polling on August 1.

Title: Kulasegaran launches Perak Bar Mediation Centre, says disputes can be settled in a day
Article:
IPOH, July 10 — Long-running and backlogged civil cases pending before the courts can now be referred to mediation service without being withdrawn, allowing disputes to be resolved in as little as one day.
Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said mediation services provide faster and more cost-effective way to resolve disputes.
Kulasegaran suggested parties involved in pending civil suits do not have to withdraw their cases to pursue mediation, but instead apply to the court for the proceedings to be stayed temporarily and seek the judge’s permission to refer the dispute to mediation.
”I think the better approach is not to withdraw the case. Let the case remain in court and have it stayed. The parties can then seek the judge’s permission to refer the matter to mediation.
”We should not encourage parties to withdraw their cases entirely. If mediation is unsuccessful, they would have to file a fresh case, which would result in more time and higher costs,” he told reporters after launching the Perak Bar Mediation Centre (PBMC) in Medan Istana here.
Kulasegaran said the approach would allow disputing parties to attempt an amicable settlement while preserving their existing court proceedings if mediation fails.
He noted that civil litigation often stretches over many years because of the multiple stages involved in the judicial process, including appeals.
”In litigation, it is not just about winning at the High Court. There can be an appeal and even a further appeal. There are several stages, and I have personally handled cases that lasted 10 to 15 years.
”Mediation enables disputes to be resolved much earlier. It provides a win-win outcome for both parties, and that is why it should be given priority,” he said.
Kulasegaran added that mediation offers a less formal and less confrontational environment than court proceedings, making it easier for parties to negotiate and reach mutually acceptable solutions.
”It is less formal, and the atmosphere is much better than the hostile environment of a courtroom,” he said.
Asked whether pending civil cases across the country could be referred to mediation, Kulasegaran said the option is available and judges are generally supportive of such applications.
”Yes. All pending civil cases in courts throughout the country can be referred to mediation. Judges would be more than happy to allow it,” he said.
Mediation centres handle a wide range of civil disputes, including family matters, employment issues, contractual and commercial disputes, personal injury claims, property disputes, and medical negligence claims.
The centres promote mediation as a confidential and cost-effective alternative to litigation, offering quicker resolution of disputes while allowing parties to decide on mutually agreed settlements.
Agreements reached through mediation are also legally binding, providing certainty and finality for those involved.
At some mediation centres, such as the PBMC, the cost for the mediation service is between RM3,000 to RM5,000 which is about three times cheaper than the cost of litigation.
On May 18, the government also officially launched the Madani Mediation Centre in Kuala Lumpur offering free assistance to the B40 and M40 groups.

Title: Seberang Perai council upgrades 70 playgrounds, plans to revitalise 630 more neighbourhood parks
Article:
SEBERANG PERAI, July 10 — The Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) has upgraded about 70 neighbourhood playgrounds in housing estates over the past three years, with plans to continue upgrading the remaining 630 open spaces.
Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said MBSP currently manages about 700 open spaces in existing housing estates across Seberang Perai.
He said these open spaces were handed over by developers of the housing estates many years ago without playgrounds or recreational amenities.
“So, the city council has to upgrade these open spaces to include recreational facilities such as playgrounds for the communities,” he told reporters at the launch of a newly upgraded playground in Taman Mangga in Bukit Tengah here today.
